schema.appDescription: Calculate Insured Declared Value (IDV) and estimated premium for your car or two-wheeler. IDV is the maximum claim amount in case of total loss or theft.

inputs.label: Vehicle Age (Years)
inputs.hint: 0 = brand new, up to 5 years considered

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Motor Insurance IDV Calculator. Smart defaults are pre-filled.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is IDV in car insurance?
faq.a: IDV (Insured Declared Value) is the maximum amount an insurer pays for total loss (write-off or theft) of your vehicle. It's the current market value of the vehicle — ex-showroom price minus depreciation. IDV decreases each year as the car depreciates. Lower IDV = lower premium but less payout in a total loss.

faq.q: How is IDV calculated in India?
faq.a: IDV = Ex-showroom price × (1 − Depreciation Rate). Depreciation schedule: <6 months: 5%, 6m–1yr: 15%, 1–2yr: 20%, 2–3yr: 30%, 3–4yr: 40%, 4–5yr: 50%. Above 5 years: IDV is mutually agreed with insurer. IRDAI sets these depreciation rates.

faq.q: Should I declare a lower IDV to reduce premium?
faq.a: Reducing IDV reduces premium but directly reduces your claim payout in a total loss. A 10% IDV reduction might save ₹3,000 in annual premium but reduces your claim by ₹1–2 lakh. Always ensure IDV reflects actual market value. Many insurers allow IDV to be adjusted ±20%.
No. All calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹12 lakh car aged 2 years — IDV calculation
examples.text: IDV at 2 years = ₹12 lakh × (1 − 20%) = ₹9.6 lakh. Own damage premium ≈ 2–3.5% of IDV = ₹19,200–₹33,600. Third party premium (1000–1500cc): ₹4,188/year (IRDAI regulated). Total comprehensive premium: ₹23,000–₹38,000.

examples.heading: 5-year-old car — renew or let lapse?
examples.text: 5-year-old car at 50% depreciation: ₹15 lakh car → IDV ₹7.5 lakh. If replacement cost is only ₹8–9 lakh (resale), a ₹7.5 lakh IDV is still worth insuring. Premium: approximately ₹15,000–₹25,000. Always renew comprehensive insurance — third-party is legally mandatory.
